coreIssue: This growth conversation is really about whether Ahmed can define and step into Head-of-Product-level strategic ownership instead of defaulting to tactical delivery.
openingQuestion: What kind of strategic ownership would you want to handle with more confidence as you grow beyond your current Head of Product scope?
listenFor: whether he names a specific decision, forum, or cross-functional outcome that he believes sits at the next Head of Product level
if they pivot from strategic scope into sprint, roadmap, or execution detail instead of describing influence, decision rights, or business outcomes
whether he identifies a specific stakeholder or peer relationship where trust feels thin and describes a recent moment, not a general impression
avoid: do not let the conversation become a critique of his current delivery habits without defining the strategic scope he is trying to grow into
do not give him a broad list of leadership behaviours that is not tied to Head of Product decision authority and leverage
goodOutcome: You and Ahmed have agreed one Head-of-Product-level scope he will own more end to end, with a shared definition of the stakeholder influence and delegation that make it senior-level work.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent example where Ahmed moved into tactical delivery and one example where he operated at strategic Head of Product scope so you can contrast them concretely.
